EVRAZ HAS RECEIVED A SPECIAL LICENCE FROM OFSI FOR AGM2022
17 June 2022 - EVRAZ plc (LSE: EVR or the "Company") informs that the Company has received a special licence from the Office of Financial Sanctions Implementation (OFSI).
The licence permits the Company to hold the 2022 Annual General Meeting (“AGM”) at Pasley-Tyler & Co. Ltd, 42 Berkeley Square, London W1J 5AW at 11:00 a.m. (London time) on Thursday 30 June 2022.
Notice of the AGM and Form of Proxy for the AGM are available at:
https://www.evraz.com/en/investors/shareholders/gm/

latest.title: EVRAZ plc publishes its Modern Slavery Act Transparency Statement for 2021
latest.content:
28 Jun 2022 15:45
EVRAZ plc (EVR)
EVRAZ plc publishes its Modern Slavery Act Transparency Statement for 2021
28-Jun-2022 / 17:45 MSK
Dissemination of a Regulatory Announcement that contains inside information according to REGULATION (EU) No 596/2014 (MAR), transmitted by EQS Group.
The issuer is solely responsible for the content of this announcement.
EVRAZ plc publishes its Modern Slavery Act Transparency Statement for 2021
EVRAZ plc (LSE: EVR) (the "Company") has published today its Modern Slavery Act Transparency Statement (the "Statement") for the year ended 31 December 2021.
The Statement is prepared in accordance with the requirements of the UK Modern Slavery Act 2015 and sets out the steps taken by the Company throughout its global business to identify and reduce the risk of modern slavery.
The Report is available on website at the following link:
https://www.evraz.com/en/sustainability/data-center/regulatory-reports/#tabs-2021

latest.content:
30 Jun 2022 13:55
EVRAZ plc (EVR)
EVRAZ plc: Results of the Annual General Meeting
30-Jun-2022 / 15:55 MSK
Dissemination of a Regulatory Announcement that contains inside information according to REGULATION (EU) No 596/2014 (MAR), transmitted by EQS Group.
The issuer is solely responsible for the content of this announcement.
EVRAZ
plc (the “Company”)
Results of the Annual General Meeting
The Annual General Meeting (the “Meeting”) of the Company was held on Thursday 30 June 2022 at 11.00am (London time). At the Meeting, the ordinary and special resolutions set out in the Notice of the Annual General Meeting dated 8 June 2022 (the “Notice of AGM”), were proposed and passed by way of a poll. Resolutions 1 to 7 were passed as ordinary resolutions. Resolution 8 was passed as special resolution.
Full details of the poll results are set out below and will also be available on the Company’s website
www.evraz.com

A "vote withheld" is not a vote under English law and is not counted in the calculation of the proportion of votes "for" or "against" a resolution.
The issued share capital of EVRAZ plc as at 30 June 2022 is 1,506,527,294 ordinary shares. The Company holds 47,837,582 ordinary shares in treasury. The total number of voting rights attaching to the ordinary shares of the Company is therefore 1,458,689,712.
The scrutineer of the poll was Computershare Investor Services PLC.
All percentages are rounded to two decimal points.

company_30s.what_they_do: EVR is a holding vehicle that owns a stake in the Russian steel giant EVRAZ and a handful of other related assets. It doesn’t run any operations of its own; its purpose is to channel capital and manage the ownership structure of those underlying businesses.
company_30s.why_they_matter: EVRAZ is one of the world’s largest steel producers, supplying key industrial markets from Europe to Asia. Because of its size and the strategic importance of steel, any change in EVRAZ’s ownership or regulatory status ripples through the global supply chain and can shift investor sentiment dramatically.
company_30s.current_state: EVR is effectively a shell in a state of limbo. The company’s shares are trading at pennies, and its board has been reshuffling amid sanctions and asset‑transfer discussions. The business is not generating revenue; it’s simply holding a portfolio that may be frozen or transferred. Investors see a company that is waiting for a regulatory or ownership decision to unlock value.

rns_narrative: The bulk of recent filings revolve around EVRAZ’s debt management, board changes, and the tightening of sanctions. The company is negotiating the transfer of a majority of its Russian assets to a new shell, and the UK regulator is reviewing whether the listing should be suspended.

A second theme is the legal and financial restructuring of EVRAZ’s European and US bond portfolios. These moves signal attempts to keep the company afloat and preserve shareholder value despite the sanctions.

Finally, there are sporadic disclosures about the termination of services and the removal of certain directors, suggesting that the corporate structure is being streamlined for compliance and potential relisting.

crowd_tape.bull_narrative: A few traders believe the asset transfer will unlock value and that the shares will relist at a premium once sanctions ease. They see the current price as a bargain for a future upside.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Most market participants fear that the company will never get a clean exit – the assets may stay frozen and the listing could be wound down, leaving shareholders with a worthless paper.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The chatter is quiet but growing in intensity when new regulatory news drops. The mood swings from cautious optimism to bleak pessimism, but the overall volume remains low.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends is flat – there is no public buzz, which is exactly what contrarian traders look for in a surprise play.

near_term_catalysts: A formal announcement from the UK regulator that the listing will be reinstated or that the shell has received a licence to trade again.
A court or government decision that releases the frozen EVRAZ assets back to the shell, allowing the company to monetize its stake.
Any major corporate event such as a bond refinancing or a capital raise that signals the company is moving from a shell to an active entity.
expect_next.watch_for: Key signals are a sudden spike in trading volume, a news release about a sanctions lift, or a change in the company’s filing status (e.g., from ‘suspended’ to ‘active’). A sharp move above the last support level would also confirm that the market is starting to price in the asset transfer.
expect_next.timing_regime: The shares are in the early to mid‑phase of a deep crash cycle – the market has already capitulated and the price is at a bottom. A mean‑reversion bounce is likely once a catalyst clears the regulatory fog.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: The stock has plunged far below the price it traded at before sanctions. If the shell finally receives the rights to the EVRAZ assets, the share price could swing back to 50‑100% of its pre‑sanction level. The upside is potentially 3–4× the current price, while the downside is limited to the current market value.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: If the company is delisted or if the sanctions never lift, the shares could stay stagnant or fall further to a price floor that is essentially zero. The worst outcome is a total wipe‑out of the investment.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Risk: 1 unit (the current market price). Reward: 3–4 units if the asset transfer triggers a rebound. The risk‑to‑reward ratio is roughly 1:3, making it an attractive asymmetric play if the catalyst materialises.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter when a regulatory decision or asset‑transfer announcement is made and the price closes above the 20‑day moving average. Size the position so that a 20‑pence drop would stop you – this protects against a brief dip while you wait for the bounce.
playbook.if_watching: Hold a small position until you see a volume spike of at least 2‑3× the average daily volume or a news release about sanctions easing. Use a tight stop below the recent low to limit loss.
playbook.if_passing: If the company is officially delisted or the regulator announces a permanent suspension, exit immediately. There is no realistic upside if the shares become non‑tradeable.

ai_insights.bottom_line: EVR is a deep‑crashed shell holding a key steel asset; with no public chatter, the next regulatory decision or asset transfer could trigger a 3‑to‑4‑fold rebound, making it a high‑asymmetry contrarian play.
